target: data/wins.json WIN-045/046 fixes + data/sections.json part2 SEC-2.2 tidal section strengthening
kernel_of_truth: The dome model correctly identifies the five major tidal constituents and their periods. Tidal periodicity tied to lunar and solar orbital timescales is real. The dome's recognition that tides require a forcing mechanism with specific timing distinguishes it from naive flat-earth models that ignore tides entirely. However, correctly listing known tidal periods is necessary but not sufficient for a tidal model — a complete theory must also predict amplitudes, spatial patterns, and the two-bulge structure.

patches.description: Fix duplicate parenthetical in tidal force variation note
patches.old_text: (first-order approximation; the exact near-far ratio is ~10%) (first-order approximation; the exact near-far ratio is ~10%)
patches.new_text: (first-order gravitational force approximation; tidal differential force varies by ~10%)

patches.description: Fix 'thermal spike' → 'tidal spike' for consistency with gravitational argument
patches.old_text: Period is trivial (half a solar day); dome's local sun at 5,733 km produces sharp thermal spike, not the observed two-bulge pattern
patches.new_text: Period is trivial (half a solar day); dome's local sun at 5,733 km produces sharp tidal spike, not the observed two-bulge pattern

patches.description: Clarify gravitational vs tidal force scaling and add amphidromic points + tidal timing arguments
patches.context: After the existing 'Anticipated objection — aetheric tides' paragraph, add new strengthening arguments. Also fix the force description from 'tidal force varies by only ~6.6%' to clarify this is gravitational force.

replacements.old_text: the moon is 384,400 km away — about 60× Earth's radius. This means tidal force varies by only ~6.6% across Earth's entire diameter.
replacements.new_text: the moon is 384,400 km away — about 60× Earth's radius. This means gravitational force varies by only ~6.6% across Earth's diameter (tidal differential force, which scales as 1/r³, varies by ~10% — still nearly uniform).

replacements.old_text: has explained the clock but not the physics.
replacements.new_text:
has explained the clock but not the physics.

The timing problem cuts deeper still: the dome model claims M2, S2, K1, O1, and N2 as predictions — but these periods are mathematically derived from gravitational forcing by a distant moon and sun in Doodson's harmonic expansion. If the tidal mechanism is aetheric rather than gravitational, it is unexplained why aetheric forcing produces timing signatures identical to gravitational tidal theory to four or more decimal places. The dome implicitly accepts gravitational tidal timing while rejecting gravitational tidal forcing.

The spatial fingerprint: amphidromic points. Real ocean tides exhibit amphidromic points — zero-amplitude nodes with cotidal lines radiating outward — which arise from the Coriolis deflection of two opposing tidal bulges rotating around ocean basins. A single-spike forcing from a nearby moon would produce radial wave propagation from the sub-lunar point, with fundamentally different nodal structure. The observed amphidromic pattern is independently confirmable via global tide gauge networks and is a geometric fingerprint of two-bulge forcing that no parameter adjustment can replicate from a single-spike source.

patches.description: DOCX uses 4.5.4 for tidal section while HTML uses 2.2. This is a build-pipeline issue for the decider/tinker, not an analyst content issue. DOCX section numbering should be reconciled with HTML post-V6 restructure.
patches.note: The curmudgeon flagged DOCX vs HTML section numbering mismatch (DOCX: 4.5.4, HTML: 2.2). This pattern appears in multiple sections post-V6 restructure. Recommend batch fix in build-doc-v4.js to apply V6 numbering to DOCX output. Already tracked in EXP-122/ISS-808.

new_evidence_added: Amphidromic points as independent geometric fingerprint of two-bulge forcing (Pugh & Woodworth 2014, NOAA CO-OPS)
Doodson timing argument: dome implicitly accepts gravitational timing while rejecting mechanism
Tidal force scaling clarification: gravitational force 1/r² (~6.6%) vs tidal differential 1/r³ (~10%)

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome might claim aetheric tides produce amphidromic points through a different mechanism
anticipated_objections.response: Amphidromic points arise from Coriolis deflection of two-bulge forcing in rotating ocean basins. The specific pattern (node locations, cotidal line orientations) is predicted by spherical tidal theory and confirmed by observations. A single-spike forcing would produce radial propagation patterns with different nodal structure. The dome would need to specify the aetheric mechanism and derive amphidromic locations from dome geometry — it has never attempted this.

Both figures support the same argument (nearly uniform forcing on the globe vs extreme spike on the dome). But a physics-literate dome defender could challenge '6.6% tidal force' as using the wrong power law. Clarifying preempts this and actually strengthens the argument — the tidal variation is ~10%, still far below the dome's hundreds-of-percent variation.
